extra.reasoning: The mutual-exclusion property NOT(rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b) is violated. rec_drug_a is set true only by rule rec_a_from_conjunction, which requires heart_failure AND hepatic_impairment AND renal_impairment AND qt_prolongation. rec_drug_b is set true only by rule rec_b_from_trigger, which requires heart_failure. Therefore every input that makes rec_drug_a true (heart_failure must be among its four required conjuncts) also satisfies the trigger for rec_drug_b. No rule sets either output false, and the default only applies to outputs left unset, so no priority/order interaction prevents both from firing (they target different outputs, so priorities never compete). Concrete counterexample: heart_failure=true, hepatic_impairment=true, renal_impairment=true, qt_prolongation=true, asthma=false, immunosuppressed=false. rec_a_from_conjunction fires -> rec_drug_a=true; rec_b_from_trigger fires -> rec_drug_b=true; both jointly true, violating the property.
